message.content: Adversaries may exploit the macOS feature that reopens applications upon user logon to achieve persistence. By modifying plist files, specifically the com.apple.loginwindow.[UUID].plist within the ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost directory, they can ensure a malicious application is automatically executed when a user logs in. This technique leverages the macOS GUI prompt that asks users if they want to "Reopen windows when logging back in," adding currently open applications to the plist file. The potential impact includes unauthorized execution of malicious payloads, leading to persistent access and further system compromise. Common exploitation methods involve adversaries adding their malicious application paths to the plist file. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting modifications to plist files, educating users about the risks of reopening applications, and employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to detect and block unauthorized changes to autostart configurations.
